Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3112:     // Returns the set of reflow phases run as a [`ReflowPhasesRun`].\\n3113:     pub(crate) fn update_the_rendering(&self) -> ReflowPhasesRun {\\n3114:         if self.render_blocking_element_count() > 0 {\\n3115:             return Default::default();\\n3116:         }\\n3117: \\n3118:         let mut results = ReflowPhasesRun::empty();\\n3119:         if self.has_pending_animated_image_update.get() {\\n3120:             self.image_animation_manager\\n3121:                 .borrow()\\n3122:                 .update_active_frames(&self.window, self.current_animation_timeline_value());\\n3123:             self.has_pending_animated_image_update.set(false);\\n3124:             results.insert(ReflowPhasesRun::UpdatedImageData);\\n3125:         }\\n3126: \\n3127:         self.current_rendering_epoch\\n3128:             .set(self.current_rendering_epoch.get().next());\\n3129:         let current_rendering_epoch = self.current_rendering_epoch.get();\\n3130: \\n3131:         // All dirty canvases are flushed before updating the rendering.\\n3132:         let image_keys: Vec<_> = self\\n3133:             .dirty_canvases\\n3134:             .borrow_mut()\\n3135:             .drain(..)\\n3136:             .filter_map(|canvas| canvas.update_rendering(current_rendering_epoch))\\n3137:             .collect();\\n3138: \\n3139:         // The renderer should wait to display the frame until all canvas images are\\n3140:         // uploaded. This allows canvas image uploading to happen asynchronously.\\n3141:         let pipeline_id = self.window().pipeline_id();\\n3142:         if !image_keys.is_empty() {\\n3143:             results.insert(ReflowPhasesRun::UpdatedImageData);\\n3144:             self.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates.set(true);\\n3145:             self.window().paint_api().delay_new_frame_for_canvas(\\n3146:                 self.webview_id(),\\n3147:                 self.window().pipeline_id(),\\n3148:                 current_rendering_epoch,\\n3149:                 image_keys,\\n3150:             );\\n3151:         }\\n3152: \\n3153:         let results = results.union(self.window().reflow(ReflowGoal::UpdateTheRendering));\\n3154: \\n3155:         self.window().paint_api().update_epoch(\\n3156:             self.webview_id(),\\n3157:             pipeline_id,\\n3158:             current_rendering_epoch,\\n3159:         );\\n3160: \\n3161:         results\\n3162:     }\\n3163: \\n3164:     pub(crate) fn handle_no_longer_waiting_on_asynchronous_image_updates(&self) {\\n3165:         self.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates.set(false);\\n3166:     }\\n3167: \\n3168:     pub(crate) fn waiting_on_canvas_image_updates(&self) -> bool {\\n3169:         self.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates.get()\\n3170:     }\\n3171: \\n3172:     /// From <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-font-loading/#fontfaceset-pending-on-the-environment>:\\n3173:     ///\\n3174:     /// > A FontFaceSet is pending on the environment if any of the following are true:\\n3175:     /// >  - the document is still loading\\n3176:     /// >  - the document has pending stylesheet requests\\n3177:     /// >  - the document has pending layout operations which might cause the user agent to request\\n3178:     /// >    a font, or which depend on recently-loaded fonts\\n3179:     ///\\n3180:     /// Returns true if the promise was fulfilled.\\n3181:     pub(crate) fn maybe_fulfill_font_ready_promise(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> bool {\\n3182:         if !self.is_fully_active() {\\n3183:             return false;\\n3184:         }\\n3185: \\n3186:         let fonts = self.Fonts(can_gc);\\n3187:         if !fonts.waiting_to_fullfill_promise() {\\n3188:             return false;\\n3189:         }\\n3190:         if self.window().font_context().web_fonts_still_loading() != 0 {\\n3191:             return false;\\n3192:         }\\n3193:         if self.ReadyState() != DocumentReadyState::Complete {\\n3194:             return false;\\n3195:         }\\n3196:         if !self.restyle_reason().is_empty() {\\n3197:             return false;\\n3198:         }\\n3199:         if !self.rendering_update_reasons.get().is_empty() {\\n3200:             return false;\\n3201:         }\\n3202: \\n3203:         let result = fonts.fulfill_ready_promise_if_needed(can_gc);\\n3204: \\n3205:         // Add a rendering update after the `fonts.ready` promise is fulfilled just for\\n3206:         // the sake of taking screenshots. This has the effect of delaying screenshots\\n3207:         // until layout has taken a shot at updating the rendering.\\n3208:         if result {\\n3209:             self.add_rendering_update_reason(RenderingUpdateReason::FontReadyPromiseFulfilled);\\n3210:         }\\n3211: \\n3212:         result\\n3213:     }\\n3214: \\n3215:     pub(crate) fn id_map(\\n3216:         &self,\\n3217:     ) -> Ref<'_, HashMapTracedValues<Atom, Vec<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>> {\\n3218:         self.id_map.borrow()\\n3219:     }\\n3220: \\n3221:     pub(crate) fn name_map(\\n3222:         &self,\\n3223:     ) -> Ref<'_, HashMapTracedValues<Atom, Vec<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>> {\\n3224:         self.name_map.borrow()\\n3225:     }\\n3226: \\n3227:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#dom-resizeobserver-resizeobserver>\\n3228:     pub(crate) fn add_resize_observer(&self, resize_observer: &ResizeObserver) {\\n3229:         self.resize_observers\\n3230:             .borrow_mut()\\n3231:             .push(Dom::from_ref(resize_observer));\\n3232:     }\\n3233: \\n3234:     /// Whether or not this [`Document`] has any active [`ResizeObserver`].\\n3235:     pub(crate) fn has_resize_observers(&self) -> bool {\\n3236:         !self.resize_observers.borrow().is_empty()\\n3237:     }\\n3238: \\n3239:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#gather-active-observations-h>\\n3240:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#has-active-resize-observations>\\n3241:     pub(crate) fn gather_active_resize_observations_at_depth(\\n3242:         &self,\\n3243:         depth: &ResizeObservationDepth,\\n3244:     ) -> bool {\\n3245:         let mut has_active_resize_observations = false;\\n3246:         for observer in self.resize_observers.borrow_mut().iter_mut() {\\n3247:             observer.gather_active_resize_observations_at_depth(\\n3248:                 depth,\\n3249:                 &mut has_active_resize_observations,\\n3250:             );\\n3251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL3112-3250\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3112:     // Returns the set of reflow phases run as a [`ReflowPhasesRun`].\n3113:     pub(crate) fn update_the_rendering(&self) -> ReflowPhasesRun {\n3114:         if self.render_blocking_element_count() > 0 {\n3115:             return Default::default();\n3116:         }\n3117: \n3118:         let mut results = ReflowPhasesRun::empty();\n3119:         if self.has_pending_animated_image_update.get() {\n3120:             self.image_animation_manager\n3121:                 .borrow()\n3122:                 .update_active_frames(&self.window, self.current_animation_timeline_value());\n3123:             self.has_pending_animated_image_update.set(false);\n3124:             results.insert(ReflowPhasesRun::UpdatedImageData);\n3125:         }\n3126: \n3127:         self.current_rendering_epoch\n3128:             .set(self.current_rendering_epoch.get().next());\n3129:         let current_rendering_epoch = self.current_rendering_epoch.get();\n3130: \n3131:         // All dirty canvases are flushed before updating the rendering.\n3132:         let image_keys: Vec<_> = self\n3133:             .dirty_canvases\n3134:             .borrow_mut()\n3135:             .drain(..)\n3136:             .filter_map(|canvas| canvas.update_rendering(current_rendering_epoch))\n3137:             .collect();\n3138: \n3139:         // The renderer should wait to display the frame until all canvas images are\n3140:         // uploaded. This allows canvas image uploading to happen asynchronously.\n3141:         let pipeline_id = self.window().pipeline_id();\n3142:         if !image_keys.is_empty() {\n3143:             results.insert(ReflowPhasesRun::UpdatedImageData);\n3144:             self.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates.set(true);\n3145:             self.window().paint_api().delay_new_frame_for_canvas(\n3146:                 self.webview_id(),\n3147:                 self.window().pipeline_id(),\n3148:                 current_rendering_epoch,\n3149:                 image_keys,\n3150:             );\n3151:         }\n3152: \n3153:         let results = results.union(self.window().reflow(ReflowGoal::UpdateTheRendering));\n3154: \n3155:         self.window().paint_api().update_epoch(\n3156:             self.webview_id(),\n3157:             pipeline_id,\n3158:             current_rendering_epoch,\n3159:         );\n3160: \n3161:         results\n3162:     }\n3163: \n3164:     pub(crate) fn handle_no_longer_waiting_on_asynchronous_image_updates(&self) {\n3165:         self.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates.set(false);\n3166:     }\n3167: \n3168:     pub(crate) fn waiting_on_canvas_image_updates(&self) -> bool {\n3169:         self.waiting_on_canvas_image_updates.get()\n3170:     }\n3171: \n3172:     /// From <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-font-loading/#fontfaceset-pending-on-the-environment>:\n3173:     ///\n3174:     /// > A FontFaceSet is pending on the environment if any of the following are true:\n3175:     /// >  - the document is still loading\n3176:     /// >  - the document has pending stylesheet requests\n3177:     /// >  - the document has pending layout operations which might cause the user agent to request\n3178:     /// >    a font, or which depend on recently-loaded fonts\n3179:     ///\n3180:     /// Returns true if the promise was fulfilled.\n3181:     pub(crate) fn maybe_fulfill_font_ready_promise(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> bool {\n3182:         if !self.is_fully_active() {\n3183:             return false;\n3184:         }\n3185: \n3186:         let fonts = self.Fonts(can_gc);\n3187:         if !fonts.waiting_to_fullfill_promise() {\n3188:             return false;\n3189:         }\n3190:         if self.window().font_context().web_fonts_still_loading() != 0 {\n3191:             return false;\n3192:         }\n3193:         if self.ReadyState() != DocumentReadyState::Complete {\n3194:             return false;\n3195:         }\n3196:         if !self.restyle_reason().is_empty() {\n3197:             return false;\n3198:         }\n3199:         if !self.rendering_update_reasons.get().is_empty() {\n3200:             return false;\n3201:         }\n3202: \n3203:         let result = fonts.fulfill_ready_promise_if_needed(can_gc);\n3204: \n3205:         // Add a rendering update after the `fonts.ready` promise is fulfilled just for\n3206:         // the sake of taking screenshots. This has the effect of delaying screenshots\n3207:         // until layout has taken a shot at updating the rendering.\n3208:         if result {\n3209:             self.add_rendering_update_reason(RenderingUpdateReason::FontReadyPromiseFulfilled);\n3210:         }\n3211: \n3212:         result\n3213:     }\n3214: \n3215:     pub(crate) fn id_map(\n3216:         &self,\n3217:     ) -> Ref<'_, HashMapTracedValues<Atom, Vec<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>> {\n3218:         self.id_map.borrow()\n3219:     }\n3220: \n3221:     pub(crate) fn name_map(\n3222:         &self,\n3223:     ) -> Ref<'_, HashMapTracedValues<Atom, Vec<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>> {\n3224:         self.name_map.borrow()\n3225:     }\n3226: \n3227:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#dom-resizeobserver-resizeobserver>\n3228:     pub(crate) fn add_resize_observer(&self, resize_observer: &ResizeObserver) {\n3229:         self.resize_observers\n3230:             .borrow_mut()\n3231:             .push(Dom::from_ref(resize_observer));\n3232:     }\n3233: \n3234:     /// Whether or not this [`Document`] has any active [`ResizeObserver`].\n3235:     pub(crate) fn has_resize_observers(&self) -> bool {\n3236:         !self.resize_observers.borrow().is_empty()\n3237:     }\n3238: \n3239:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#gather-active-observations-h>\n3240:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#has-active-resize-observations>\n3241:     pub(crate) fn gather_active_resize_observations_at_depth(\n3242:         &self,\n3243:         depth: &ResizeObservationDepth,\n3244:     ) -> bool {\n3245:         let mut has_active_resize_observations = false;\n3246:         for observer in self.resize_observers.borrow_mut().iter_mut() {\n3247:             observer.gather_active_resize_observations_at_depth(\n3248:                 depth,\n3249:                 &mut has_active_resize_observations,\n3250:             );\n3251: ",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"927: \\n928:     fn dispatch_scroll_event(\\n929:         &self,\\n930:         external_id: ExternalScrollId,\\n931:         hit_test_result: PaintHitTestResult,\\n932:     ) {\\n933:         let event = InputEvent::Scroll(EmbedderScrollEvent { external_id }).into();\\n934:         let msg = EmbedderToConstellationMessage::ForwardInputEvent(\\n935:             self.id,\\n936:             event,\\n937:             Some(hit_test_result),\\n938:         );\\n939:         if let Err(e) = self.embedder_to_constellation_sender.send(msg) {\\n940:             warn!(\\\"Sending scroll event to constellation failed ({:?}).\\\", e);\\n941:         }\\n942:     }\\n943: \\n944:     pub(crate) fn pinch_zoom(&self) -> PinchZoom {\\n945:         self.pinch_zoom\\n946:     }\\n947: \\n948:     fn set_pinch_zoom(&mut self, requested_pinch_zoom: PinchZoom) -> PinchZoomResult {\\n949:         if requested_pinch_zoom == self.pinch_zoom {\\n950:             return PinchZoomResult::DidNotPinchZoom;\\n951:         }\\n952: \\n953:         self.pinch_zoom = requested_pinch_zoom;\\n954:         PinchZoomResult::DidPinchZoom\\n955:     }\\n956: \\n957:     pub(crate) fn set_page_zoom(\\n958:         &mut self,\\n959:         new_page_zoom: Scale<f32, CSSPixel, DeviceIndependentPixel>,\\n960:     ) {\\n961:         let new_page_zoom = new_page_zoom.clamp(MIN_PAGE_ZOOM, MAX_PAGE_ZOOM);\\n962:         let old_zoom = std::mem::replace(&mut self.page_zoom, new_page_zoom);\\n963:         if old_zoom != self.page_zoom {\\n964:             self.send_window_size_message();\\n965:         }\\n966:     }\\n967: \\n968:     /// The scale to use when displaying this [`WebViewRenderer`] in WebRender\\n969:     /// including both viewport scale (page zoom and hidpi scale) as well as any\\n970:     /// pinch zoom applied. This is based on the latest display list received,\\n971:     /// as page zoom changes are applied asynchronously and the rendered view\\n972:     /// should reflect the latest display list.\\n973:     pub(crate) fn device_pixels_per_page_pixel(&self) -> Scale<f32, CSSPixel, DevicePixel> {\\n974:         let viewport_scale = self\\n975:             .root_pipeline_id\\n976:             .and_then(|pipeline_id| self.pipelines.get(&pipeline_id))\\n977:             .and_then(|pipeline| pipeline.viewport_scale)\\n978:             .unwrap_or_else(|| self.page_zoom * self.hidpi_scale_factor);\\n979:         viewport_scale * self.pinch_zoom.zoom_factor()\\n980:     }\\n981: \\n982:     /// The current viewport scale (hidpi scale and page zoom and not pinch\\n983:     /// zoom) based on the current setting of the WebView. Note that this may\\n984:     /// not be the rendered viewport zoom as that is based on the latest display\\n985:     /// list and zoom changes are applied asynchronously.\\n986:     pub(crate) fn device_pixels_per_page_pixel_not_including_pinch_zoom(\\n987:         &self,\\n988:     ) -> Scale<f32, CSSPixel, DevicePixel> {\\n989:         self.page_zoom * self.hidpi_scale_factor\\n990:     }\\n991: \\n992:     /// Adjust the pinch zoom of the [`WebView`] by the given zoom delta.\\n993:     pub(crate) fn adjust_pinch_zoom(&mut self, magnification: f32, center: DevicePoint) {\\n994:         if magnification == 1.0 {\\n995:             return;\\n996:         }\\n997: \\n998:         self.pending_scroll_zoom_events\\n999:             .push(ScrollZoomEvent::PinchZoom(magnification, center));\\n1000:     }\\n1001: \\n1002:     fn send_window_size_message(&self) {\\n1003:         // The device pixel ratio used by the style system should include the scale from page pixels\\n1004:         // to device pixels, but not including any pinch zoom.\\n1005:         let device_pixel_ratio = self.device_pixels_per_page_pixel_not_including_pinch_zoom();\\n1006:         let initial_viewport = self.rect.size().to_f32() / device_pixel_ratio;\\n1007:         let _ = self.embedder_to_constellation_sender.send(\\n1008:             EmbedderToConstellationMessage::ChangeViewportDetails(\\n1009:                 self.id,\\n1010:                 ViewportDetails {\\n1011: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/paint/webview_renderer.rs%3FL927-1010\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "927: \n928:     fn dispatch_scroll_event(\n929:         &self,\n930:         external_id: ExternalScrollId,\n931:         hit_test_result: PaintHitTestResult,\n932:     ) {\n933:         let event = InputEvent::Scroll(EmbedderScrollEvent { external_id }).into();\n934:         let msg = EmbedderToConstellationMessage::ForwardInputEvent(\n935:             self.id,\n936:             event,\n937:             Some(hit_test_result),\n938:         );\n939:         if let Err(e) = self.embedder_to_constellation_sender.send(msg) {\n940:             warn!(\"Sending scroll event to constellation failed ({:?}).\", e);\n941:         }\n942:     }\n943: \n944:     pub(crate) fn pinch_zoom(&self) -> PinchZoom {\n945:         self.pinch_zoom\n946:     }\n947: \n948:     fn set_pinch_zoom(&mut self, requested_pinch_zoom: PinchZoom) -> PinchZoomResult {\n949:         if requested_pinch_zoom == self.pinch_zoom {\n950:             return PinchZoomResult::DidNotPinchZoom;\n951:         }\n952: \n953:         self.pinch_zoom = requested_pinch_zoom;\n954:         PinchZoomResult::DidPinchZoom\n955:     }\n956: \n957:     pub(crate) fn set_page_zoom(\n958:         &mut self,\n959:         new_page_zoom: Scale<f32, CSSPixel, DeviceIndependentPixel>,\n960:     ) {\n961:         let new_page_zoom = new_page_zoom.clamp(MIN_PAGE_ZOOM, MAX_PAGE_ZOOM);\n962:         let old_zoom = std::mem::replace(&mut self.page_zoom, new_page_zoom);\n963:         if old_zoom != self.page_zoom {\n964:             self.send_window_size_message();\n965:         }\n966:     }\n967: \n968:     /// The scale to use when displaying this [`WebViewRenderer`] in WebRender\n969:     /// including both viewport scale (page zoom and hidpi scale) as well as any\n970:     /// pinch zoom applied. This is based on the latest display list received,\n971:     /// as page zoom changes are applied asynchronously and the rendered view\n972:     /// should reflect the latest display list.\n973:     pub(crate) fn device_pixels_per_page_pixel(&self) -> Scale<f32, CSSPixel, DevicePixel> {\n974:         let viewport_scale = self\n975:             .root_pipeline_id\n976:             .and_then(|pipeline_id| self.pipelines.get(&pipeline_id))\n977:             .and_then(|pipeline| pipeline.viewport_scale)\n978:             .unwrap_or_else(|| self.page_zoom * self.hidpi_scale_factor);\n979:         viewport_scale * self.pinch_zoom.zoom_factor()\n980:     }\n981: \n982:     /// The current viewport scale (hidpi scale and page zoom and not pinch\n983:     /// zoom) based on the current setting of the WebView. Note that this may\n984:     /// not be the rendered viewport zoom as that is based on the latest display\n985:     /// list and zoom changes are applied asynchronously.\n986:     pub(crate) fn device_pixels_per_page_pixel_not_including_pinch_zoom(\n987:         &self,\n988:     ) -> Scale<f32, CSSPixel, DevicePixel> {\n989:         self.page_zoom * self.hidpi_scale_factor\n990:     }\n991: \n992:     /// Adjust the pinch zoom of the [`WebView`] by the given zoom delta.\n993:     pub(crate) fn adjust_pinch_zoom(&mut self, magnification: f32, center: DevicePoint) {\n994:         if magnification == 1.0 {\n995:             return;\n996:         }\n997: \n998:         self.pending_scroll_zoom_events\n999:             .push(ScrollZoomEvent::PinchZoom(magnification, center));\n1000:     }\n1001: \n1002:     fn send_window_size_message(&self) {\n1003:         // The device pixel ratio used by the style system should include the scale from page pixels\n1004:         // to device pixels, but not including any pinch zoom.\n1005:         let device_pixel_ratio = self.device_pixels_per_page_pixel_not_including_pinch_zoom();\n1006:         let initial_viewport = self.rect.size().to_f32() / device_pixel_ratio;\n1007:         let _ = self.embedder_to_constellation_sender.send(\n1008:             EmbedderToConstellationMessage::ChangeViewportDetails(\n1009:                 self.id,\n1010:                 ViewportDetails {\n1011: ",

{

custom event</h1>\\n14:     <h4>Test Description: This test checks if on pointer event handlers through attributes works properly using synthetic pointerevents. For valid results, this test must be run without generating real (trusted) events on the black rectangle below.</h4>\\n15:     <div id=\\\"target0\\\"\\n16:          onpointercancel=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointercancel';\\\"\\n17:          onpointerdown=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointerdown';\\\"\\n18:          onpointerup=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointerup';\\\"\\n19:          onpointermove=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointermove';\\\"\\n20:          onpointerout=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointerout';\\\"\\n21:          onpointerover=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointerover';\\\"\\n22:          onpointerleave=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointerleave';\\\"\\n23:          onpointerenter=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'pointerenter';\\\"\\n24:          ongotpointercapture=\\\"window.eventHappened = 'gotpointercapture';\\\"\\n25: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":27,\"startLine\":12},{\"content\":\"27:     <script>\\n28:         window.eventHappened = '';\\n29: \\n30:         All_Pointer_Events.forEach(function(event) {\\n31:             var on_event = \\\"on\\\" + event;\\n32: \\n33: \",\"endLine\":33,\"startLine\":27}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointerevent_on_event_handlers.html\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/pointerevents/pointerevent_on_event_handlers.html\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"22: <script>\\n23:   window.onload = async () => {\\n24:     async function pan_viewport_test(add_event_listener_func) {\\n25:       const preScrollVisualViewportOffsetTop = visualViewport.offsetTop;\\n26:       const preScrollWindowScrollOffset = window.scrollY;\\n27: \\n28:       const scrollend_promise = add_event_listener_func();\\n29: \\n30:       const scrollAmount = 50;\\n31:       await touchScrollInTarget(scrollAmount, document.documentElement, \\\"up\\\");\\n32:       await scrollend_promise;\\n33: \\n34: \",\"endLine\":34,\"startLine\":22},{\"content\":\"51:       });\\n52:     }, \\\"scrollend listener added via addEventlistener fires when the visual \\\" +\\n53:     \\\"viewport is panned.\\\");\\n54: \",\"endLine\":54,\"startLine\":51},{\"content\":\"34:       assert_less_than(visualViewport.offsetTop,\\n35:         preScrollVisualViewportOffsetTop,\\n36:         `visualViewport should be scrolled.`);\\n37:       assert_equals(window.scrollY, preScrollWindowScrollOffset,\\n38:         \\\"the window should not scroll.\\\");\\n39:       // No need to undo scroll; subsequent test has room to scroll further.\\n40:     }\\n41: \",\"endLine\":41,\"startLine\":34}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/dom/events/scrolling/scrollend-event-fires-on-visual-viewport.html\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/dom/events/scrolling/scrollend-event-fires-on-visual-viewport.html\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"80:         });\\n81:         document.addEventListener(\\\"lostpointercapture\\\", function(event){\\n82:           logMessage(\\\"Test Passed! shadow_dom_test){\\n74:             shadow_dom_test.step(function(){\\n75:               assert_unreached(\\\"lostpointercapture must be fired on the document, not the capturing element\\\");\\n76: waitForEvent(\\\"scroll\\\", t, target);\\n91:   target.scrollTo({left: frameDocEl.scrollWidth});\\n92:   await promiseForScrollX;\\n93:   assert_greater_than(target.scrollX, 0);\\n94: \\n95:         "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "10:     Test Description: This test checks that the appearance of classic\n11:     scrollbars will cause a resize event to be fired at window.visualViewport in iframe.\n12: </h4>\n13: ",
            "endLine": 13,
            "startLine": 10
          },
          {
            "content": "37:     }\n38:   }, \"the appearance of classic scrollbars will fire a resize event\" +\n39:       \"at window.visualViewport in iframe\");\n40: </script>\n41: ",
            "endLine": 41,
            "startLine": 37
          },
          {
            "content": "15:   async_test(t => {\n16:     window.onload = () => {\n17:       const iframe = document.querySelector(\"iframe\");\n18: \n19:       let resize_event_count = 0;\n20:       iframe.contentWindow.visualViewport.addEventListener(\"resize\", () => {\n21:         resize_event_count++;\n22:       });\n23: ",
            "endLine": 23,
            "startLine": 15
          }
        ],
        "file": "tests/wpt/tests/visual-viewport/viewport-scrollbars-cause-resize-in-iframe.html",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/visual-viewport/viewport-scrollbars-cause-resize-in-iframe.html"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "17: promise_test(async t => {\n18:   const rc = await addWindow();\n19:   const waitForPort = expectMessagePortFromWindow(window);\n20:   await createMessageChannelAndSendPort(rc);\n21:   const closeEventPromise = createCloseEventPromise(await waitForPort);\n22:   rc.executeScript(() => window.close());\n23:   await closeEventPromise;\n24: }, 'The window is closed and a close event is fired.')\n25: \n26: ",
            "endLine": 26,
            "startLine": 17
          },
          {
            "content": "8: promise_test(async t => {\n9:   const rc = await addWindow();\n10:   const waitForPort = expectMessagePortFromWindow(window);\n11:   await createMessageChannelAndSendPort(rc);\n12:   const closeEventPromise = createCloseEventPromise(await waitForPort);\n13:   rc.navigateToNew();\n14:   await closeEventPromise;\n15: }, 'The context is navigated to a new document and a close event is fired.')\n16: \n17: ",
            "endLine": 17,
            "startLine": 8
          },
          {
            "content": "37:   port1.start();\n38:   iframe.contentWindow.postMessage('', '*', [port2]);\n39:   await waitForPort;\n40:   const closeEventPromise = createCloseEventPromise(port1);\n41:   iframe.remove();\n42:   await closeEventPromise;\n43: }, 'The iframe is deleted and a close event is fired.')\n44: ",
            "endLine": 44,
            "startLine": 37
          }
        ],
        "file": "tests/wpt/tests/webmessaging/message-channels/close-event/document-destroyed.tentative.window.js",

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "14:         Test Description: This test checks that the appearance of classic\n15:         scrollbars will cause a resize event to be fired at window.visualViewport.\n16:     </h4>\n17: ",
            "endLine": 17,
            "startLine": 14
          },
          {
            "content": "33:         var t = async_test(\n34:             \"Resize event was fired at window.visualViewport if, and only if, \" +\n35:             \"scrollbars are classic (i.e. affect flow)\");\n36:         var viewResized = false;\n37:         window.visualViewport.addEventListener('resize', function() {\n38:             viewResized = true;\n39: ",
            "endLine": 39,
            "startLine": 33

assert_greater_than(document.scrollingElement.scrollLeft, target_div.clientWidth - 1,\\n103:         \\\"document is scrolled by the height of target_div\\\");\\n104:     }, \\\"testing, horizontal\\\");\\n105:   }\\n106: </script>\\n107: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/dom/events/scrolling/scrollend-event-fired-to-document.html\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: <!DOCTYPE HTML>\n2: <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1,minimum-scale=1\">\n3: <script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n4: <script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n5: <script src=\"/resources/testdriver.js\"></script>\n6: <script src=\"/resources/testdriver-actions.js\"></script>\n7: <script src=\"/resources/testdriver-vendor.js\"></script>\n8: <script src=\"scroll_support.js\"></script>\n9: <style>\n10:   #hspacer {\n11:     height: 100px;\n12:     width: 100vw;\n13:     top: 0;\n14:     left: 200px;\n15:     /* on the right edge od targetDiv */\n16:     position: absolute;\n17:   }\n18: \n19:   #vspacer {\n20:     height: 100vh;\n21:     width: 100px;\n22:     position: absolute;\n23:   }\n24: \n25:   #targetDiv {\n26:     width: 200px;\n27:     height: 200px;\n28:     overflow: scroll;\n29:   }\n30: \n31:   #innerDiv {\n32:     width: 400px;\n33:     height: 400px;\n34:   }\n35: </style>\n36: \n37: <body style=\"margin:0\" onload=runTest()>\n38:   <div id=\"targetDiv\">\n39:     <div id=\"innerDiv\"></div>\n40:   </div>\n41:   <div id=\"hspacer\"></div>\n42:   <div id=\"vspacer\"></div>\n43: </body>\n44: \n45: <script>\n46:   var target_div = document.getElementById('targetDiv');\n47:   async function resetScrollers(test) {\n48:     await waitForScrollReset(test, target_div);\n49:     await waitForScrollReset(test, document.scrollingElement);\n50:   }\n51: \n52:   function fail() {\n53:     assert_true(false);\n54:   }\n55: \n56:   function runTest() {\n57:     promise_test(async (t) => {\n58:       await resetScrollers(t);\n59:       await waitForCompositorCommit();\n60: \n61:       assert_equals(document.scrollingElement.scrollTop, 0,\n62:         \"document should not be scrolled\");\n63: \n64:       let scrollend_promise = waitForScrollendEvent(t, target_div);\n65:       let max_target_div_scroll_top = target_div.scrollHeight - target_div.clientHeight;\n66:       target_div.scrollTo({ top: target_div.scrollHeight, left: 0 });\n67:       await scrollend_promise;\n68:       assert_approx_equals(target_div.scrollTop, max_target_div_scroll_top, 1,\n69:         \"target_div should be fully scrolled down\");\n70: \n71:       scrollend_promise = waitForScrollendEvent(t, document, 2000);\n72:       target_div.addEventListener(\"scrollend\", fail);\n73:       // Scroll up on target div and wait for the doc to get scrollend event.\n74:       await scrollElementDown(target_div, target_div.clientHeight + 25);\n75:       await scrollend_promise;\n76: \n77:       target_div.removeEventListener(\"scrollend\", fail);\n78:       assert_greater_than(document.scrollingElement.scrollTop, target_div.clientHeight - 1,\n79:         \"document is scrolled by the height of target_div\");\n80:     }, \"testing, vertical\");\n81: \n82:     promise_test(async (t) => {\n83:       await resetScrollers(t);\n84:       await waitForCompositorCommit();\n85: \n86:       assert_equals(document.scrollingElement.scrollLeft, 0,\n87:         \"document should not be scrolled\");\n88: \n89:       let scrollend_promise = waitForScrollendEvent(t, target_div);\n90:       let max_target_div_scroll_left = target_div.scrollWidth - target_div.clientWidth;\n91:       target_div.scrollTo({ left: target_div.scrollWidth, top: 0 });\n92:       await scrollend_promise;\n93:       assert_approx_equals(target_div.scrollLeft, max_target_div_scroll_left, 1,\n94:         \"target_div should be fully scrolled right\");\n95: \n96:       scrollend_promise = waitForScrollendEvent(t, document, 2000);\n97:       target_div.addEventListener(\"scrollend\", fail);\n98:       await scrollElementLeft(target_div, target_div.clientWidth + 25);\n99:       await scrollend_promise;\n100: \n101:       target_div.removeEventListener(\"scrollend\", fail);\n102:       assert_greater_than(document.scrollingElement.scrollLeft, target_div.clientWidth - 1,\n103:         \"document is scrolled by the height of target_div\");\n104:     }, \"testing, horizontal\");\n105:   }\n106: </script>\n107: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/dom/events/scrolling/scrollend-event-fired-to-document.html"
  }
}
32. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Servo Scrollend Event Implementation\n\n## Current Understanding\n\n### Scroll Event Architecture in Servo\n- Scroll events are handled through \"pending scroll event targets\" list in Document\n- When scroll occurs, `handle_viewport_scroll_event()` or `handle_element_scroll_event()` adds targets to pending list\n- During `update_the_rendering()`, the pending targets are processed\n- The actual Event firing happens in the script thread\n\n### Key Files\n- `components/script/dom/document.rs`: Implements scroll event handling and pending list\n- `components/script/dom/document_event_handler.rs`: Handles input scroll events from embedder\n- `components/script/dom/window.rs`: Window scroll methods\n- `components/script/dom/event.rs`: Base Event class\n- `components/paint/webview_renderer.rs`: Dispatches scroll events from paint side\n\n### What Tests Expect\n- `scrollend` events should fire on elements after `.scrollTo()` completes\n- `scrollend` events should fire on window/document after user scroll completes\n- Events must fire with proper properties (non-cancelable, doesn't bubble on elements)\n- Multiple rapid scrolls should result in a single scrollend (debouncing)\n- Event should not fire if scroll position didn't actually change\n\n### Implementation Strategy\n1. Add scrollend event creation and firing alongside scroll events\n2. Implement debouncing mechanism (150-200ms of no scroll activity)\n3. Track scroll position to detect if actually changed\n4. Fire events through existing Event system\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Servo Scrollend Event Implementation\n\n## Current Understanding\n\n### Scroll Event Architecture in Servo\n- Scroll events are handled through \"pending scroll event targets\" list in Document\n- When scroll occurs, `handle_viewport_scroll_event()` or `handle_element_scroll_event()` adds targets to pending list\n- During `update_the_rendering()`, the pending targets are processed\n- The actual Event firing happens in the script thread\n\n### Key Files\n- `components/script/dom/document.rs`: Implements scroll event handling and pending list\n- `components/script/dom/document_event_handler.rs`: Handles input scroll events from embedder\n- `components/script/dom/window.rs`: Window scroll methods\n- `components/script/dom/event.rs`: Base Event class\n- `components/paint/webview_renderer.rs`: Dispatches scroll events from paint side\n\n### What Tests Expect\n- `scrollend` events should fire on elements after `.scrollTo()` completes\n- `scrollend` events should fire on window/document after user scroll completes\n- Events must fire with proper properties (non-cancelable, doesn't bubble on elements)\n- Multiple rapid scrolls should result in a single scrollend (debouncing)\n- Event should not fire if scroll position didn't actually change\n\n### Implementation Strategy\n1. Add scrollend event creation and firing alongside scroll events\n2. Implement debouncing mechanism (150-200ms of no scroll activity)\n3. Track scroll position to detect if actually changed\n4. Fire events through existing Event system\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

}

are negative, if within document scrollable area');\\n29: </script>\\n30: \",\"endLine\":79,\"startLine\":72}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/dom/events/scrolling/scrollend-event-not-fired-on-no-scroll.html\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/dom/events/scrolling/scrollend-event-not-fired-on-no-scroll.html\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"23:   // This should be triggered first.\\n24:   window.addEventListener('load', t.step_func(() => {\\n25:     has_window_load_fired = true;\\n26:     // Scroll the loading=lazy below-viewport image into view, so that it loads.\\n27:     image.scrollIntoView();\\n28:   }));\\n29: \",\"endLine\":29,\"startLine\":23},{\"content\":\"17:   const t = async_test(\\\"Images with loading='lazy' can be lazy loaded multiple times\\\");\\n18:   const image = document.querySelector('#below_viewport');\\n19:   const top_div = document.querySelector('#top_div');\\n20: \\n21:   let has_window_load_fired = false;\\n22: \\n23: \",\"endLine\":23,\"startLine\":17},{\"content\":\"30:   image.onload = t.step_func(() => {\\n31:     assert_true(has_window_load_fired,\\n32:                 \\\"The loading=lazy below-viewport image should not block the \\\" +\\n33:                 \\\"window load event\\\");\\n34:     changeImageSourceAndScrollToTop();\\n35:   });\\n36: \",\"endLine\":36,\"startLine\":30}],\"file\":\"tests/wpt/tests/html/semantics/embedded-content/the-img-element/image-loading-lazy-multiple-times.html\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99/-/blob/tests/wpt/tests/html/semantics/embedded-content/the-img-element/image-loading-lazy-multiple-times.html\"}]}",

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "28: <script>\n29: var target_div = document.getElementById('targetDiv');\n30: var window_received_overscroll = false;\n31: \n32: function onOverscroll(event) {\n33:   assert_false(event.cancelable);\n34:   // overscroll events targetting document are bubbled to the window.\n35:   assert_true(event.bubbles);\n36:   window_received_overscroll = true;\n37: }\n38: window.addEventListener(\"overscroll\", onOverscroll);\n39: \n40: ",
            "endLine": 40,
            "startLine": 28
          },
          {
            "content": "41:   promise_test (async (t) => {\n42:     // Make sure that no overscroll event is sent to target_div.\n43:     target_div.addEventListener(\"overscroll\",\n44:         t.unreached_func(\"target_div got unexpected overscroll event.\"));\n45:     // Scroll up on target div and wait for the window to get overscroll event.\n46:     await touchScrollInTarget(300, target_div, 'up');\n47:     await waitFor(() => { return window_received_overscroll; },\n48:         'Window did not receive overscroll event after scroll up on target.');\n49:   }, 'Tests that the window gets overscroll event when no element scrolls' +\n50:      'after touch scrolling.');\n51: }\n52: ",
            "endLine": 52,
            "startLine": 41
          },
          {
            "content": "11:   height: 200px;\n12:   overflow: scroll;\n13: }\n14: ",

     * Test workflow:\\n15:      * - Create preconnect link\\n16:      * - Wait 200 ms\\n17:      * - Create preload link\\n18:      * Event listeners for 'load' and 'error' are attached to both links.\\n19:      * The first event listener that fires is tested to see which element fired it.\\n20:      * The event should always be fired by the preload element because per spec, the preconnect link should never fire these events:\\n21:      * https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#link-type-preconnect\\n22:      *\\n23:      * This test assumes that the preload link element fires 'load' and 'error'             "content": "3:     <head>\n4:         <title>Pointer Event: gotPointercapture is fired first.</title>\n5:         <meta content=\"text/html; charset=UTF-8\" http-equiv=\"Content-Type\" />\n6:         <link rel=\"author\" title=\"Microsoft\" href=\"http://www.microsoft.com/\" />\n7:         <link rel=\"help\" href=\"http://www.w3.org/wiki/PointerEvents/TestAssertions\">\n8:         <meta name=\"assert\" content=\"When the setPointerCapture method is invoked, if the provided pointerId value does not match any of the active pointers, a DOMException with the name NotFountError must be thrown.\" />\n9:         <link rel=\"stylesheet\" type=\"text/css\" href=\"pointerevent_styles.css\">\n10:         <!-- /resources/testharness.js -->\n11: ",

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "14:      * Test workflow:\n15:      * - Create preconnect link\n16:      * - Wait 200 ms\n17:      * - Create preload link\n18:      * Event listeners for 'load' and 'error' are attached to both links.\n19:      * The first event listener that fires is tested to see which element fired it.\n20:      * The event should always be fired by the preload element because per spec, the preconnect link should never fire these events:\n21:      * https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#link-type-preconnect\n22:      *\n23:      * This test assumes that the preload link element fires 'load' and 'error' events correctly, otherwise the test will timeout.\n24:      */\n25: ",
            "endLine": 25,
            "startLine": 14
          },
          {
            "content": "33:                     link.rel = rel;\n34:                     link.addEventListener('load', () => didLoad({rel, type: 'load'}));\n35:                     link.addEventListener('error', () => didLoad({rel, type: 'error'}));\n36:                     document.head.appendChild(link);\n37: ",
            "endLine": 37,
            "startLine": 33
          },
          {
            "content": "43: \n44:     test_preconnect(HTTP_REMOTE_ORIGIN, '/preload/resources/dummy.js', 'Preconnect should not fire load (or error) events');\n45:     test_preconnect('http://NON-EXISTENT.origin', '/preload/resources/dummy.js', 'Preconnect should not fire error (or load) events for non-existent origins');\n46:     test_preconnect('some-scheme://URL', '/preload/resources/dummy.js', 'Preconnect should not fire error (or load) events for non-http(s) scheme');\n47: </script>\n48: ",
            "endLine": 48,
            "startLine": 43
          }
        ],

      },

            "content": "31: \n32:   assert_equals(target.scrollTop, 0);\n33:   var promiseForScrollTop = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n34:   target.scrollTop = 10;\n35:   await promiseForScrollTop;\n36:   assert_equals(target.scrollTop, 10);\n37: \n38:   assert_equals(target.scrollLeft, 0);\n39:   var promiseForScrollLeft = waitForEvent(\"scroll\", t, target);\n40:   target.scrollLeft = 10;\n41:   await promiseForScrollLeft;\n42:   assert_equals(target.scrollLeft, 10);\n43: \n44: }, \"scrollTop and scrollLeft should fire scroll event.\");\n45: \n46: ",

35. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_keyword_search @ -
Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99$ \"scroll\" Event type DomString"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"11: [Exposed=Window]\\n12: interface SnapEvent : Event {\\n13:   constructor(DOMString type, optional SnapEventInit eventInitDict = {});\\n14: Output

  "content": "{\"content\":\"930:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-event-fire>\\n931:     pub(crate) fn fire_event_with_params(\\n932:         &self,\\n933:         name: Atom,\\n934:         bubbles: EventBubbles,\\n935:         cancelable: EventCancelable,\\n936:         composed: EventComposed,\\n937:         can_gc: CanGc,\\n938:     ) -> bool {\\n939:         let event = Event::new(&self.global(), name, bubbles, cancelable, can_gc);\\n940:         event.set_composed(composed.into());\\n941:         event.fire(self, can_gc)\\n942:     }\\n943: \\n944:     /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-eventtarget-addeventlistener>\\n945:     /// and <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#add-an-event-listener>\\n946:     pub(crate) fn add_event_listener(\\n947:         &self,\\n948:         ty: DOMString,\\n949:         listener: Option<Rc<EventListener>>,\\n950:         options: AddEventListenerOptions,\\n951:     ) {\\n952:         if let Some(signal) = options.signal.as_ref() {\\n953:             // Step 2. If listener\u2019s signal is not null and is aborted, then return.\\n954:             if signal.aborted() {\\n955:                 return;\\n956:             }\\n957:             // Step 6. If listener\u2019s signal is not null, then add the following abort steps to it:\\n958:             signal.add(&AbortAlgorithm::DomEventListener(\\n959:                 RemovableDomEventListener {\\n960:                     event_target: Dom::from_ref(self),\\n961:                     ty: ty.clone(),\\n962:                     listener: listener.clone(),\\n963:                     options: options.parent.clone(),\\n964:                 },\\n965:             ));\\n966:         }\\n967:         // Step 3. 40. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 1720,
  "path": "components/script/dom/document.rs",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99",
  "startLine": 1690
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1690:     pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\\n1691:         // Step 1.\\n1692:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\\n1693:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1694: \\n1695:         // Step 2\\n1696:         // > For each item target in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\\n1697:         // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\\n1698:         // Step 3.\\n1699:         // > Empty doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\\n1700:         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\\n1701:         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\\n1702:         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\\n1703:         for target in notify_list.iter() {\\n1704:             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\\n1705:                 // Step 2.1\\n1706:                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\\n1707:                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\\\"scroll\\\"), can_gc);\\n1708:             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\\n1709:                 // Step 2.2\\n1710:                 // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\\n1711:                 target.fire_event(Atom::from(\\\"scroll\\\"), can_gc);\\n1712:             }\\n1713:         }\\n1714: \\n1715:         // Step 4.\\n1716:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\\n1717:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\\n1718:     }\\n1719: \\n1720:     /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\\n1721: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1690-1720\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1690:     pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n1691:         // Step 1.\n1692:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\n1693:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n1694: \n1695:         // Step 2\n1696:         // > For each item target in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\n1697:         // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\n1698:         // Step 3.\n1699:         // > Empty doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\n1700:         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\n1701:         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\n1702:         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n1703:         for target in notify_list.iter() {\n1704:             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n1705:                 // Step 2.1\n1706:                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\n1707:                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n1708:             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n1709:                 // Step 2.2\n1710:                 // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\n1711:                 target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n1712:             }\n1713:         }\n1714: \n1715:         // Step 4.\n1716:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\n1717:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n1718:     }\n1719: \n1720:     /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n1721: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/script/dom/document.rs%3FL1690-1720"
  }
}

Input
{
  "command": "git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/servo/servo.git 2>&1 | head -20",
  "description": "Clone the Servo repository to work with"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "            let title = self.title().map(String::from);\n            self.send_to_embedder(EmbedderMsg::ChangePageTitle(self.webview_id(), title));\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn send_to_embedder(&self, msg: EmbedderMsg) {\n        let window = self.window();\n        window.send_to_embedder(msg);\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn dirty_all_nodes(&self) {\n        let root = match self.GetDocumentElement() {\n            Some(root) => root,\n            None => return,\n        };\n        for node in root\n            .upcast::<Node>()\n            .traverse_preorder(ShadowIncluding::Yes)\n        {\n            node.dirty(NodeDamage::Other)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-run-the-scroll-steps>\n    pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // Step 1.\n        // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\n        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n\n        // Step 2\n        // > For each item target in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\n        // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\n        // Step 3.\n        // > Empty doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\n        // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\n        // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\n        rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n        for target in notify_list.iter() {\n            if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n                // Step 2.1\n                // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\n                target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n                // Step 2.2\n                // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\n                target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.\n        // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\n        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n    }\n\n    /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>\n    pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {\n        // Step 2.\n        // > If doc is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging targets\n        // > for doc with doc\u2019s eventual snap target in the block axis as newBlockTarget and\n        // > doc\u2019s eventual snap target in the inline axis as newInlineTarget.\n        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n\n        // Step 3.\n        // > If doc is already in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.\n        let target = self.upcast::<EventTarget>();\n        if self\n            .pending_scroll_event_targets\n            .borrow()\n            .iter()\n            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.\n        // > Append doc to doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\n        self.pending_scroll_event_targets\n            .borrow_mut()\n            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n    }\n\n    /// Whenever an element gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>\n    pub(crate) fn handle_element_scroll_event(&self, element: &Element) {\n        // Step 2.\n        // > If the element is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging\n        // > targets for the element with the element\u2019s eventual snap target in the block\n        // > axis as newBlockTarget and the element\u2019s eventual snap target in the inline axis\n        // > as newInlineTarget.\n        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n\n        // Step 3.\n        // > If the element is already in doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.\n        let target = element.upcast::<EventTarget>();\n        if self\n            .pending_scroll_event_targets\n            .borrow()\n            .iter()\n            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.\n        // > Append the element to doc\u2019s pending scroll event targets.\n        self.pending_scroll_event_targets\n            .borrow_mut()\n            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n    }\n\n    // https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#converting-nodes-into-a-node\n    pub(crate) fn node_from_nodes_and_strings(\n        &self,\n        mut nodes: Vec<NodeOrString>,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Node>> {\n        if nodes.len() == 1 {\n            Ok(match nodes.pop().unwrap() {\n                NodeOrString::Node(node) => node,\n                NodeOrString::String(string) => {\n                    DomRoot::upcast(self.CreateTextNode(string, can_gc))\n                },\n            })\n        } else {\n            let fragment = DomRoot::upcast::<Node>(self.CreateDocumentFragment(can_gc));\n            for node in nodes {\n                match node {\n                    NodeOrString::Node(node) => {\n                        fragment.AppendChild(&node, can_gc)?;\n                    },\n                    NodeOrString::String(string) => {\n                        let node = DomRoot::upcast::<Node>(self.CreateTextNode(string, can_gc));\n                        // No try!() here because appending a text node\n                        // should not fail.\n                        fragment.AppendChild(&node, can_gc).unwrap();\n                    },\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(fragment)\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn get_body_attribute(&self, local_name: &LocalName) -> DOMString {\n        match self.GetBody() {\n            Some(ref body) if body.is_body_element() => {\n                body.upcast::<Element>().get_string_attribute(local_name)\n            },",

For instance see\n            // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#flush-autofocus-candidates.\n            self.process_pending_input_events(*pipeline_id, CanGc::from_cx(cx));\n\n            // > 8. For each doc of docs, run the resize steps for doc. [CSSOMVIEW]\n            let resized = document.window().run_the_resize_steps(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\n\n            // > 9. For each doc of docs, run the scroll steps for doc.\n            document.run_the_scroll_steps(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\n\n            // Media queries is only relevant when there are resizing.\n            if resized {\n                // 10. For each doc of docs, evaluate media queries and report changes for doc.\n                document\n                    .window()\n                    .evaluate_media_queries_and_report_changes(CanGc::from_cx(cx));\n\n                // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#img-environment-changes\n                // As per the spec, this can be run at any time."

{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/scrollend.patch << 'EOF'\n--- a/components/script/dom/document.rs\n+++ b/components/script/dom/document.rs\n@@ -582,6 +582,8 @@ pub(crate) struct Document {\n     adopted_stylesheets_frozen_types: CachedFrozenArray,\n     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-pending-scroll-event-targets>\n     pending_scroll_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,\n+    /// Pending scrollend event targets\n+    pending_scrollend_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,\n     /// Other reasons that a rendering update might be required for this [`Document`].\n     rendering_update_reasons: Cell<RenderingUpdateReason>,\n     /// Whether or not this [`Document`] is waiting on canvas image updates. If it is\n@@ -1717,8 +1719,20 @@ impl Document {\n         // > Empty doc's pending scroll event targets.\n         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\n         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\n         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n         for target in notify_list.iter() {\n             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n                 // Step 2.1\n                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\n                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n                 // Step 2.2\n                 // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.\n                 target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n             }\n         }\n+\n+        // Fire scrollend events after scroll events\n+        rooted_vec!(let scrollend_list <- self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n+        for target in scrollend_list.iter() {\n+            if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n+                // Fire scrollend that bubbles at document\n+                target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n+            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n+                // Fire scrollend at element (doesn't bubble)\n+                target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n+            }\n+        }\n \n         // Step 4.\n         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\n@@ -1754,6 +1768,12 @@ impl Document {\n         // Append doc to doc's pending scroll event targets.\n         self.pending_scroll_event_targets\n             .borrow_mut()\n             .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n+\n+        // Also queue for scrollend event\n+        self.pending_scrollend_event_targets\n+            .borrow_mut()\n+            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n     }\n \n     /// Whenever an element gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n@@ -1799,6 +1819,12 @@ impl Document {\n         // Append the element to doc's pending scroll event targets.\n         self.pending_scroll_event_targets\n             .borrow_mut()\n             .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n+\n+        // Also queue for scrollend event\n+        self.pending_scrollend_event_targets\n+            .borrow_mut()\n+            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n     }\n \nEOF\ncat /tmp/scrollend.patch\n",
  "description": "Create a patch for scrollend support"
}

target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n+                // Fire scrollend that bubbles at document\n+                target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n+            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n+                // Fire scrollend at element (doesn't bubble)\n+                target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n+            }\n+        }\n \n         // Step 4.\n         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\n@@ -1754,6 +1768,12 @@ impl Document {\n         // Append doc to doc's pending self.pending_scrollend_event_targets\n+            .borrow_mut()\n+            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n     }\n \n     /// Whenever an element gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n@@ -1799,6 +1819,12 @@ impl Document {\n         // Append the element to doc's pending scroll event targets.\n         self.pending_scroll_event_targets\n             .borrow_mut()\n             .push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n+\n+        // Also queue for scrollend event\n+        self.pending_scrollend_event_targets\n+            .borrow_mut()\n+ Default::default(),\n            value_override: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns a policy value that should be used for fetches initiated by this document.\n    pub(crate) fn insecure_requests_policy(&self) -> InsecureRequestsPolicy {\n        if let Some(csp_list) = self.get_csp_list().as_ref() {\n            for policy in &csp_list.0 {\n                if policy.contains_a_directive_whose_name_is(\"upgrade-insecure-requests\") &&\n                    policy.disposition == PolicyDisposition::Enforce\n                {\n                    return InsecureRequestsPolicy::Upgrade;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.inherited_insecure_requests_policy\n            .get()\n            .unwrap_or(InsecureRequestsPolicy::DoNotUpgrade)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the [`Document`]'s [`DocumentEventHandler`].\n    pub(crate) fn event_handler(&self) -> &DocumentEventHandler {\n        &self.event_handler\n    }\n\n    /// Get the [`Document`]'s [`DocumentEmbedderControls`].\n    pub(crate) fn embedder_controls(&self) -> &DocumentEmbedderControls {\n        &self.embedder_controls\n    }",

crate::timers::OneshotTimerCallback;\nuse crate::xpath::parse_expression;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]\npub(crate) enum FireMouseEventType {\n    Move,\n    Over,\n    Out,\n    Enter,\n    Leave,\n}\n\nimpl FireMouseEventType {\n    pub(crate) fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        match *self {\n            FireMouseEventType::Move => \"mousemove\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Over => \"mouseover\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Out => \"mouseout\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Enter => \"mouseenter\",\n            FireMouseEventType::Leave => \"mouseleave\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct RefreshRedirectDue {\n    #[no_trace]\n    pub(crate) url: ServoUrl,\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"non-owning\"]\n    pub(crate) window: DomRoot<Window>,\n}\nimpl RefreshRedirectDue {\n    /// Step 13 of <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#shared-declarative-refresh-steps>\n    pub(crate) fn invoke(self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // After the refresh has come due (as defined below),\n        // if the user has not canceled the redirect and, if meta is given,\n        // document's active sandboxing flag set does not have the sandboxed\n        // automatic features browsing context flag set,\n        // then navigate document's node navigable to urlRecord using document,\n        // with historyHandling set to \"replace\".\n        //\n        // TODO: check sandbox\n        // TODO: Check if meta was given\n        let load_data = self\n            .window\n            .load_data_for_document(self.url.clone(), self.window.pipeline_id());\n        self.window\n            .load_url(NavigationHistoryBehavior::Replace, false, load_data, can_gc);\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf, PartialEq)]\npub(crate) enum IsHTMLDocument {\n    HTMLDocument,\n    NonHTMLDocument,\n}\n\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n#[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\nstruct FocusTransaction {\n    /// The focused element of this document.\n    element: Option<Dom<Element>>,\n    /// See [`Document::has_focus`].\n    has_focus: bool,\n    /// Focus options for the transaction\n    focus_options: FocusOptions,\n}\n\n/// Information about a declarative refresh\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) enum DeclarativeRefresh {\n    PendingLoad {\n        #[no_trace]\n        url: ServoUrl,\n        time: u64,\n    },\n    CreatedAfterLoad,\n}\n\n/// Reasons why a [`Document`] might need a rendering update that is otherwise\n/// untracked via other [`Document`] properties.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct RenderingUpdateReason(u8);\n\nbitflags! {\n    impl RenderingUpdateReason: u8 {\n        /// When a `ResizeObserver` starts observing a target, this becomes true, which in turn is a\n        /// signal to the [`ScriptThread`] that a rendering update should happen.\n        const ResizeObserverStartedObservingTarget = 1 << 0;\n        /// When an `IntersectionObserver` starts observing a target, this becomes true, which in turn is a\n        /// signal to the [`ScriptThread`] that a rendering update should happen.\n        const IntersectionObserverStartedObservingTarget = 1 << 1;\n        /// All web fonts have loaded and `fonts.ready` promise has been fulfilled. We want to trigger\n        /// one more rendering update possibility after this happens, so that any potential screenshot\n        /// reflects the up-to-date contents.\n        const FontReadyPromiseFulfilled = 1 << 2;\n    }\n}\n\n/// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#document>\n#[dom_struct]\npub(crate) struct Document {\n    node: Node,\n    document_or_shadow_root: DocumentOrShadowRoot,\n    window: Dom<Window>,\n    implementation: MutNullableDom<DOMImplementation>,\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"type from external crate\"]\n    #[no_trace]\n    content_type: Mime,\n    last_modified: Option<String>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    encoding: Cell<&'static Encoding>,\n    has_browsing_context: bool,\n    is_html_document: bool,\n    #[no_trace]\n    activity: Cell<DocumentActivity>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#the-document%27s-address>\n    #[no_trace]\n    url: DomRefCell<ServoUrl>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#concept-document-about-base-url>\n    #[no_trace]\n    about_base_url: DomRefCell<Option<ServoUrl>>,\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"defined in selectors\"]\n    #[no_trace]\n    quirks_mode: Cell<QuirksMode>,\n    /// A helper used to process and store data related to input event handling.\n    event_handler: DocumentEventHandler,\n    /// A helper to handle showing and hiding user interface controls in the embedding layer.\n    embedder_controls: DocumentEmbedderControls,\n    /// Caches for the getElement methods. It is safe to use FxHash for these maps\n    /// as Atoms are `string_cache` items that will have the hash computed from a u32.\n    id_map: DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<Atom, Vec<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>>,\n    name_map: DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<Atom, Vec<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>>,\n    tag_map: DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<LocalName, Dom<HTMLCollection>, FxBuildHasher>>,\n    tagns_map: DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<QualName, Dom<HTMLCollection>, FxBuildHasher>>,\n    classes_map: DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<Vec<Atom>, Dom<HTMLCollection>>>,\n    images: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    embeds: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    links: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    forms: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    scripts: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    anchors: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    applets: MutNullableDom<HTMLCollection>,\n    /// Information about the `<iframes>` in this [`Document`].\n    iframes: RefCell<IFrameCollection>,\n    /// Lock use for style attributes and author-origin stylesheet objects in this document.\n    /// Can be acquired once for accessing many objects.\n    #[no_trace]\n    style_shared_lock: StyleSharedRwLock,\n    /// List of stylesheets associated with nodes in this document. |None| if the list needs to be refreshed.\n    #[custom_trace]\n    stylesheets: DomRefCell<DocumentStylesheetSet<ServoStylesheetInDocument>>,\n    stylesheet_list: MutNullableDom<StyleSheetList>,\n    ready_state: Cell<DocumentReadyState>,\n    /// Whether the DOMContentLoaded event has already been dispatched.\n    domcontentloaded_dispatched: Cell<bool>,\n    /// The state of this document's focus transaction.\n    focus_transaction: DomRefCell<Option<FocusTransaction>>,\n    /// The element that currently has the document focus context.\n    focused: MutNullableDom<Element>,\n    /// The last sequence number sent to the constellation.\n    #[no_trace]\n    focus_sequence: Cell<FocusSequenceNumber>,\n    /// Indicates whether the container is included in the top-level browsing\n    /// context's focus chain (not considering system focus). Permanently `true`\n    /// for a top-level document.\n    has_focus: Cell<bool>,\n    /// The script element that is currently executing.\n    current_script: MutNullableDom<HTMLScriptElement>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#pending-parsing-blocking-script>\n    pending_parsing_blocking_script: DomRefCell<Option<PendingScript>>,\n    /// Number of stylesheets that block executing the next parser-inserted script\n    script_blocking_stylesheets_count: Cell<u32>,\n    /// Number of elements that block the rendering of the page.\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#implicitly-potentially-render-blocking>\n    render_blocking_element_count: Cell<u32>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#list-of-scripts-that-will-execute-when-the-document-has-finished-parsing>\n    deferred_scripts: PendingInOrderScriptVec,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#list-of-scripts-that-will-execute-in-order-as-soon-as-possible>\n    asap_in_order_scripts_list: PendingInOrderScriptVec,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#set-of-scripts-that-will-execute-as-soon-as-possible>\n    asap_scripts_set: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<HTMLScriptElement>>>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#animation-frame-callback-identifier>\n    /// Current identifier of animation frame callback\n    animation_frame_ident: Cell<u32>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#list-of-animation-frame-callbacks>\n    /// List of animation frame callbacks\n    animation_frame_list: DomRefCell<VecDeque<(u32, Option<AnimationFrameCallback>)>>,\n    /// Whether we're in the process of running animation callbacks.\n    ///\n    /// Tracking this is not necessary for correctness. Instead, it is an optimization to avoid\n    /// sending needless `ChangeRunningAnimationsState` messages to `Paint`.\n    running_animation_callbacks: Cell<bool>,\n    /// Tracks all outstanding loads related to this document.\n    loader: DomRefCell<DocumentLoader>,\n    /// The current active HTML parser, to allow resuming after interruptions.\n    current_parser: MutNullableDom<ServoParser>,\n    /// The cached first `base` element with an `href` attribute.\n    base_element: MutNullableDom<HTMLBaseElement>,\n    /// The cached first `base` element, used for its target (doesn't need a href)\n    target_base_element: MutNullableDom<HTMLBaseElement>,\n    /// This field is set to the document itself for inert documents.\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#appropriate-template-contents-owner-document>\n    appropriate_template_contents_owner_document: MutNullableDom<Document>,\n    /// Information on elements needing restyle to ship over to layout when the\n    /// time comes.\n    pending_restyles: DomRefCell<FxHashMap<Dom<Element>, NoTrace<PendingRestyle>>>,\n    /// A collection of reasons that the [`Document`] needs to be restyled at the next\n    /// opportunity for a reflow. If this is empty, then the [`Document`] does not need to\n    /// be restyled.\n    #[no_trace]\n    needs_restyle: Cell<RestyleReason>,\n    /// Navigation Timing properties:\n    /// <https://w3c.github.io/navigation-timing/#sec-PerformanceNavigationTiming>\n    #[no_trace]\n    dom_interactive: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    dom_content_loaded_event_start: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    dom_content_loaded_event_end: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    dom_complete: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    top_level_dom_complete: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    load_event_start: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    load_event_end: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    unload_event_start: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    unload_event_end: Cell<Option<CrossProcessInstant>>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#concept-document-https-state>\n    #[no_trace]\n    https_state: Cell<HttpsState>,\n    /// The document's origin.\n    #[no_trace]\n    origin: MutableOrigin,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-referrer>\n    referrer: Option<String>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#target-element>\n    target_element: MutNullableDom<Element>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#concept-document-policy-container>\n    #[no_trace]\n    policy_container: DomRefCell<PolicyContainer>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#map-of-preloaded-resources>\n    #[no_trace]\n    preloaded_resources: DomRefCell<PreloadedResources>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#ignore-destructive-writes-counter>\n    ignore_destructive_writes_counter: Cell<u32>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#ignore-opens-during-unload-counter>\n    ignore_opens_during_unload_counter: Cell<u32>,\n    /// The number of spurious `requestAnimationFrame()` requests we've received.\n    ///\n    /// A rAF request is considered spurious if nothing was actually reflowed.\n    spurious_animation_frames: Cell<u8>,\n\n    /// Track the total number of elements in this DOM's tree.\n    /// This is sent to layout every time a reflow is done;\n    /// layout uses this to determine if the gains from parallel layout will be worth the overhead.\n    ///\n    /// See also: <https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/10110>\n    dom_count: Cell<u32>,\n    /// Entry node for fullscreen.\n    fullscreen_element: MutNullableDom<Element>,\n    /// Map from ID to set of form control elements that have that ID as\n    /// their 'form' content attribute. Used to reset form controls\n    /// whenever any element with the same ID as the form attribute\n    /// is inserted or removed from the document.\n    /// See <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#form-owner>\n    /// It is safe to use FxBuildHasher here as Atoms are in the string_cache\n    form_id_listener_map:\n        DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<Atom, HashSet<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    interactive_time: DomRefCell<ProgressiveWebMetrics>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    tti_window: DomRefCell<InteractiveWindow>,\n    /// RAII canceller for Fetch\n    canceller: FetchCanceller,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#throw-on-dynamic-markup-insertion-counter>\n    throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter: Cell<u64>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#page-showing>\n    page_showing: Cell<bool>,\n    /// Whether the document is salvageable.\n    salvageable: Cell<bool>,\n    /// Whether the document was aborted with an active parser\n    active_parser_was_aborted: Cell<bool>,\n    /// Whether the unload event has already been fired.\n    fired_unload: Cell<bool>,\n    /// List of responsive images\n    responsive_images: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<HTMLImageElement>>>,\n    /// Number of redirects for the document load\n    redirect_count: Cell<u16>,\n    /// Number of outstanding requests to prevent JS or layout from running.\n    script_and_layout_blockers: Cell<u32>,\n    /// List of tasks to execute as soon as last script/layout blocker is removed.\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Measuring trait objects is hard\"]\n    delayed_tasks: DomRefCell<Vec<Box<dyn NonSendTaskBox>>>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#completely-loaded>\n    completely_loaded: Cell<bool>,\n    /// Set of shadow roots connected to the document tree.\n    shadow_roots: DomRefCell<HashSet<Dom<ShadowRoot>>>,\n    /// Whether any of the shadow roots need the stylesheets flushed.\n    shadow_roots_styles_changed: Cell<bool>,\n    /// List of registered media controls.\n    /// We need to keep this list to allow the media controls to\n    /// access the \"privileged\" document.servoGetMediaControls(id) API,\n    /// where `id` needs to match any of the registered ShadowRoots\n    /// hosting the media controls UI.\n    media_controls: DomRefCell<HashMap<String, Dom<ShadowRoot>>>,\n    /// A set of dirty HTML canvas elements that need their WebRender images updated the\n    /// next time the rendering is updated.\n    dirty_canvases: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<HTMLCanvasElement>>>,\n    /// Whether or not animated images need to have their contents updated.\n    has_pending_animated_image_update: Cell<bool>,\n    /// <https://w3c.github.io/slection-api/#dfn-selection>\n    selection: MutNullableDom<Selection>,\n    /// A timeline for animations which is used for synchronizing animations.\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/web-animations/#timeline>\n    animation_timeline: DomRefCell<AnimationTimeline>,\n    /// Animations for this Document\n    animations: Animations,\n    /// Image Animation Manager for this Document\n    image_animation_manager: DomRefCell<ImageAnimationManager>,\n    /// The nearest inclusive ancestors to all the nodes that require a restyle.\n    dirty_root: MutNullableDom<Element>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#will-declaratively-refresh>\n    declarative_refresh: DomRefCell<Option<DeclarativeRefresh>>,\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#dom-document-resizeobservers-slot>\n    ///\n    /// Note: we are storing, but never removing, resize observers.\n    /// The lifetime of resize observers is specified at\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#lifetime>.\n    /// But implementing it comes with known problems:\n    /// - <https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1596992>\n    /// - <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/4518>\n    resize_observers: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<ResizeObserver>>>,\n    /// The set of all
